title: [HW][FIRRTLMemory] Handle multi-bit mask in HWMemSimImpl
body:
`HWMemSimImpl` currently assumes a 1 bit mask. This PR adds support to handle multi-bit mask.
Given a memory data width of size `dataWidth` , and `maskWidth` number of bits in the mask.
Mask granularity is defined as the number of data bits each mask bit controls. Mask granularity = `dataWidth/maskWidth`.


In this PR we can create one register for each mask bit, and create the logic to map the data to each of the registers. 
Each of the mask bit is used to guard corresponding bits of the write data into the register. 
Creating one register for each mask, makes it simpler to update the registers using bit extraction logic.

Read logic:
1. Given the address, read the value from the corresponding registers.
2. Concat all the read values and create one value for the output.

Write Logic:
1. Given the write mask, extract each bit and create single bit masks.
2. Extract `mask granularity` number of bits from the write data.
3. Each extracted bits will be written to the corresponding register guarded by the corresponding mask bit.
